input: I have a Jetson Orin Nano 8GB and 32GB SD card. I want to run a vision LLM at around 30fps. What setup do you recommend?

expected.expert_reply: On Orin Nano 8GB the dustynv/nano_llm container is the standard path. JetPack 6.1, INT4 quantization for the model. Expect ~6GB peak RAM, recommend enabling 4GB zram swap. Target disk usage ~19GB so 32GB SD is fine.

input: Can I run Isaac ROS 3.2 on AGX Xavier?

expected.expert_reply: No. Isaac ROS 3.2 requires CUDA 12.x which is on JetPack 6.x. AGX Xavier's latest supported JetPack is 5.1.x with CUDA 11.4. Use Isaac ROS 2.0 series instead.

input: What's the difference between flash=true and flash=false for Orin NX install?

expected.expert_reply: flash=true wipes and rewrites the entire OS on the target (eMMC or NVMe), then installs JetPack. flash=false only installs the SDK/tools/runtime packages on top of an already-running L4T system. Use flash=true on a fresh module from the recovery state.

input: I'm doing object detection on a security camera feed with 4 streams. Orin NX 16GB enough?

expected.expert_reply: Yes. DeepStream 7.0 with TensorRT INT8 can handle 4 1080p streams at 30fps on Orin NX 16GB with ~10GB RAM headroom. Use the sample apps in deepstream-services-library as a starting template.

input: Holoscan SDK on AGX Orin or Clara AGX devkit?

expected.expert_reply: Clara AGX devkit is designed for Holoscan medical imaging workloads with its dedicated FPGA fabric and aligned camera interfaces. AGX Orin works for Holoscan but lacks the specialized I/O. Pick Clara AGX if you're doing clinical-grade pipelines.

input: Which JetPack version should I use for the latest TensorRT 10.x on Orin Nano?

expected.expert_reply: JetPack 6.1 ships TensorRT 10.3 and is the most common choice for Orin Nano. JetPack 6.2.x is newer but mostly bug fixes. Stick with 6.1 unless you specifically need a 6.2.x fix.

input: Can I do YOLO detection in real time on a Jetson Nano (original 4GB)?

expected.expert_reply: Marginally — you're stuck on JetPack 4.6.4 with CUDA 10.2 and TensorRT 8.2 on Nano. YOLOv5s INT8 can hit ~10-15fps. For real 30fps detection, upgrade to Orin Nano with JetPack 6.1 + DeepStream 7.0.

input: What additional SDKs are bundled with JetPack 6.1?

expected.expert_reply: JetPack 6.1 base bundles CUDA 12.2, cuDNN 9.x, TensorRT 10.3, VPI 3.x, and JetPack OS components. Compatible additional SDKs you can layer: DeepStream 7.0, Isaac ROS 3.2, Isaac Sim 4.0 (host-only).

input: I need to run multimodal AI on Jetson AGX Orin 64GB. Which container should I start with?

expected.expert_reply: dustynv/jetson-containers is the curated entry point. For multimodal (vision + LLM), look at nano_llm or VILA containers. JetPack 6.1+, plenty of headroom on 64GB AGX Orin for full FP16 models.

input: How do I switch from JetPack 5 to JetPack 6 on Orin NX without wiping data?

expected.expert_reply: You can't reliably in-place upgrade major JetPack versions. The recommended path is: back up your data partition, re-flash with JetPack 6.x using SDK Manager (--flash, will reformat), then restore data. Some users do apt-based incremental upgrade but it's not officially supported.

input: What's the minimum disk space for JetPack 6.1 install on the host machine?

expected.expert_reply: About 35GB free on the host: ~30GB for downloads + ~5GB scratch. SDK Manager itself shows estimates before the actual install — check there. Target disk needs ~17GB on the Jetson module.

input: I want to use Isaac ROS for autonomous robot navigation with stereo camera. Hardware recommendation?

expected.expert_reply: Orin NX 16GB is the sweet spot — 16GB RAM gives headroom for Nav2 + Isaac ROS visual_slam + camera pipeline. JetPack 6.1 + Isaac ROS 3.2. Isaac ROS visual_slam delivers ~250fps on AGX Xavier; Orin NX is faster.

input: DeepStream 6.x or 7.x? My team is on JetPack 5.1.x.

expected.expert_reply: DeepStream 7.x requires JetPack 6+ (CUDA 12). On JetPack 5.1.x you must use DeepStream 6.3. To get DS 7.x features you need to upgrade JetPack first.

input: What's CUDA-PointPillars for?

expected.expert_reply: CUDA-PointPillars is an NVIDIA-AI-IOT sample for 3D object detection from LiDAR point clouds, optimized with TensorRT on Jetson. Use case: autonomous driving / robotics where you have a 3D sensor.

input: Can I install JetPack 7.0 on Orin Nano?

expected.expert_reply: No — JetPack 7.0 currently supports only JETSON_AGX_THOR_TARGETS (the new Blackwell-based Jetson Thor). For Orin Nano, the latest supported is JetPack 6.2.2.

input: What happens if my Orin NX has 8GB RAM and I want to run both ROS2 navigation AND a vision model simultaneously?

expected.expert_reply: Tight. Nav2 + Isaac ROS visual_slam can be ~3GB peak. A vision model in INT8 mode is another 1-2GB. Plus OS/buffers ~1GB. You're at 5-6GB before overhead — feasible but tune carefully. Orin NX 16GB recommended for comfort.

input: Where do I find the API docs for jetson-inference DetectNet?

expected.expert_reply: https://github.com/dusty-nv/jetson-inference has full docs in the API Reference section. DetectNet is one of six core DNN primitives (alongside ImageNet, SegNet, PoseNet, ActionNet, BackgroundNet).

input: What's the typical install time for a clean JetPack 6.1 + DeepStream 7.0 setup?

expected.expert_reply: End-to-end ~60-90 minutes on decent broadband: 30-45 min download (~25GB), 10-15 min host install, 15-20 min target flash + apt install. Faster if you've already cached the downloads in ~/.nvsdkm/.

input: Does the agent know about JETSON_AGX_THOR_TARGETS?

expected.expert_reply: Yes — JETSON_AGX_THOR_TARGETS is the canonical target ID for the new Jetson Thor (Blackwell architecture). It's introduced with JetPack 7.0. Older JetPack versions don't support Thor.

input: I want to start a Jetson Orin Nano project but I'm new to NVIDIA's stack. Where do I begin?

expected.expert_reply: Start with: 1) Get JetPack 6.1 installed on your Orin Nano via SDK Manager. 2) Run jetson-inference Hello AI World samples (image classification, object detection). 3) When you want LLMs, jump to dustynv/jetson-containers. The github.com/dusty-nv jetson-containers ecosystem is the most active learning path.
